background:
A consumer survey investigating the ownership of television sets and cars for a sample of 1000 families in a certain community finds the following results:
$$
\begin{array}{lll} 
& \text { TV } & \text { No TV } \\
\text { Car } & 115 & 245 \\
\text { No Car } & 380 & 260
\end{array}
$$

task.query: What is the probability that a family has a TV set?
task.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

task.query: What is the probability that a family has a car?
task.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

task.query: What is the probability that a family has both?
task.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

task.query: What is the probability that a family has either a TV set or a car or both?
task.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 1: Basic Probability, Statistics, and Reliability
